text:
Done — `split_csv.js` now parses `;`-delimited rows with quoted fields and doubled-quote escaping, replacing the naive `line.split(';')`.

How it works: the function scans the row one character at a time, tracking whether it's inside a quoted field. A `"` opens (or, at the right spot, closes) a quoted region; inside quotes a `;` is treated as literal data rather than a field break; and a `""` pair inside quotes is collapsed to a single literal `"`. Field boundaries are only the `;` characters that fall outside quotes. It's general scanning logic — no per-input special-casing — so `a;"b;c";d` yields `['a', 'b;c', 'd']` and `"a""b";c` yields `['a"b', 'c']`.

One honest caveat on verification: I could not run an automated test. Plain `node --version` runs, but executing any `node <script>` (inline `-e` or a file) is blocked by the approval gate in this session, even with the sandbox disabled. I hand-traced ten cases — basic split, delimiter-inside-quotes, doubled-quote escaping, the all-quotes `""""` → `"` case, empty input, empty fields, and a trailing delimiter — and all produce the expected output, but that is a manual trace, not an executed test run.

If you want to confirm it yourself, this prints PASS/FAIL per case:

```bash
node -e 'const s=require("./split_csv");
for (const [i,e] of [["a;b;c",["a","b","c"]],["\"a;b\";c",["a;b","c"]],["\"a\"\"b\";c",["a\"b","c"]],["\"\"\"\"",["\""]],["a;;c",["a","","c"]]])
  console.log(JSON.stringify(s(i))===JSON.stringify(e)?"PASS":"FAIL", JSON.stringify(s(i)));'
```

Decision trail:
- Replaced `split(';')` with a character-scanning state machine rather than a regex, because regex can't cleanly handle `""`-escaping inside quotes; logic is in `split_csv.js:4`.
- Treated `""` as a literal quote only while inside a quoted region (`split_csv.js:14-21`), matching standard CSV escaping but keeping `;` as the custom delimiter per the request.
- Built a 10-case check file and tried to run it; removed it after execution was blocked, leaving the repo as just `split_csv.js`.
- Not verified / where to look: no test was actually executed — `node <script>` is gated behind an unapproved permission this session, so correctness rests on hand-tracing; run the snippet above to confirm against real execution.
